Back to dashboard PDF cache audit PDF term index Metadata reader

OERF / BOST 论文 PDF 跳转库

当前收录 622 篇核心论文与开放阅读入口，其中 196 篇已缓存开放 PDF。缓存原则：只缓存 arXiv、NASA、开放许可或明确公开 PDF；Springer/ACM/Elsevier 等版权不明确的论文只提供出版社或在线 PDF 链接。

622papers / links
196cached open PDFs
162topic groups
建议阅读顺序：先读 NeRIF、PIV-BOST、4D BOST 三篇何远哲主线，再读 NeDF/NRIP/open BOS dataset 作为方法邻居，最后读 Computational Flow Visualization 和 BOS 综述补总框架。
术语辅助阅读：公开缓存 PDF 会生成“术语导读/页码地图”，按单篇论文列出核心术语、解释、易混点、页码跳转、按页术语地图和跨论文反查，尽量接近维基百科式的读论文导航，但不复制论文正文。

受限全文访问队列私有库边界说明

must He mainline cached PDF

Neural refractive index field: Unlocking the Potential of Background-oriented Schlieren Tomography in Volumetric Flow Visualization

Yuanzhe He, Yutao Zheng, Shijie Xu, Chang Liu, Di Peng, Yingzheng Liu, Weiwei Cai

Physics of Fluids / arXiv · 2025 · 10.1063/5.0250899

Why: 何远哲主线核心；定义 NeRIF 问题、神经隐式折射率场、BOST forward model 和重投影一致性。

Extract: 公式链条、网络表示、loss、实验设置、视角数和误差指标。

Instantaneous refractive index compensation on the velocity measurement using simultaneous PIV-BOST

Yutao Zheng, Yuanzhe He, Jingruo Chen, Minshuo Li, Shijie Xu, Bi Wen, Yingzheng Liu, Weiwei Cai

Experiments in Fluids · 2025 · 10.1007/s00348-025-04093-y

Why: PIV-BOST 升级线核心；把 BOST 折射率场用于补偿 PIV 速度测量误差。

Extract: 补偿公式、同步实验设置、误差指标、2D/PIV 与 BOST 坐标关系。

must He mainline online link

Tensor Decomposition-Based Four-dimensional Background-Oriented Schlieren Tomography for High-Speed, High-Fidelity Flow Field Reconstruction

Yuanzhe He, Yutao Zheng, Shijie Xu, Ning Liu, Yingzheng Liu, Weiwei Cai

ACM Transactions on Graphics · 2026 · 10.1145/3809488

Why: 何远哲 4D BOST 最新挑战线；正式全文给出 MM tensor decomposition、完整 ray-displacement forward model、DC network、time-resolved experiment 和显存/速度消融。

Extract: MM plane pairs、3x128 Swish decoder、6x200 distortion-correction MLP、DeepFlow displacement、TV/L1/boundary loss、9-view synthetic validation、500-frame flame experiment、13.08 GB mixed-precision peak memory、paper-vs-code config mismatch。

ACM marks the Version of Record Open Access under CC BY 4.0. Official HTML full text and 19-page / 29.4 MB eReader were browser-verified on 2026-07-10; raw PDF requests without the browser session still return Cloudflare/CDN 403, so no local PDF is cached.

support PIV-BOST extension online link

Instantaneous refractive index compensation on stereo-velocity measurement in turbulent combustion

Yutao Zheng, Yuanzhe He, Lianhao Feng, Shijie Xu, Ning Liu, Miaosheng He, Hong Liu, Yingzheng Liu, Weiwei Cai

Proceedings of the Combustion Institute · 2026 · 10.1016/j.proci.2026.106175

Why: PIV-BOST 的 stereo-velocity 后续；直接说明何远哲方向已经从二维速度补偿推进到更复杂的立体速度测量误差补偿。

Extract: stereo-PIV 系统误差、折射率场补偿层级、同步测量链路、本科阶段为什么只做 2D toy 或误差传播。

Minshuo Li, Jingruo Chen, Yuanzhe He, Yutao Zheng, Shijie Xu, Ning Liu, Weiwei Cai

Flow, Turbulence and Combustion · 2026 · 10.1007/s10494-026-00737-z

Why: 何远哲参与的反应流/氨甲烷湍流预混火焰测量旁支；说明他和 OERF 真实燃烧实验链路也有交集。

Why: 4D BOST / time-resolved BOS 的外部邻居；可帮助理解何远哲 4D BOST 为什么强调高速流、时序一致性和 NIRT/uncertainty。

NeDF: neural deflection fields for sparse-view tomographic background oriented Schlieren

Jiawei Li, Xuhui Meng, Yuan Xiong, Tong Jia, Chong Pan, Jinjun Wang

Physics of Fluids / arXiv · 2024 · 10.1063/5.0241191

Why: NeRIF 的最重要外部邻居；用 neural deflection field、positional encoding 和 hierarchical sampling 处理 sparse-view TBOS。

Extract: field 表示差异、采样策略、与传统 TBOS 的对比、可复现实验轴；补充材料含 error metrics、超参数和网络结构。

Supplement Number 1 for Neural deflection field for sparse-view tomographic background oriented Schlieren

Jiawei Li, Xuhui Meng, Yuan Xiong, Tong Jia, Chong Pan, Jinjun Wang

AIP Figshare · 2024 · 10.60893/figshare.pof.27606522.v1

Why: NeDF 是 NeRIF 的核心竞品，补充材料直接给 error metrics、training hyperparameters 和 network structures，是未来做 NeRIF-vs-NeDF 复现/对比表时最实用的入口。

Extract: MSE/MAE/PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS/relative error definitions、learning rate、iteration count、positional encoding、network depth and width。

must Neural tomography neighbor cached PDF

Neural Refractive Index Primitives for Flame Field Reconstruction Using Background-Oriented Schlieren

Xinyi Lu, Wei Hu, Zizhou Liao, Zheng Wang, Yue Zhang, Jingxuan Li

Combustion and Flame / arXiv · 2026 · 10.1016/j.combustflame.2026.115082

Why: Close external neighbor to He Yuanzhe's NeRIF route: it keeps refractive index as the neural primitive, adds multiresolution hash encoding, automatic/discrete gradient losses, and a 3D mask, then evaluates on numerical combustion phantoms and real flame BOS data.

Extract: NRIP vs NeRIF/voxel baselines, hash encoding, automatic-discrete gradient loss, 3D mask, flame/BOS real-data evaluation, noise robustness, what can be reproduced as an undergraduate benchmark without touching lab-private data.

cached open arXiv PDF; formal Combustion and Flame DOI verified via Crossref. The paper reports a GitHub repository on the first page; prior GitHub API checks found no license, and the repository was not publicly reachable in the latest direct check, so code is structure-reference only.。本地已缓存PDF。

Experiments in Fluids / arXiv · 2026 · 10.1007/s00348-026-04189-z

Extract: 数据结构、视角数、NIRT baseline、九视角结果、公开代码/数据入口。

support BOS system error cached PDF

Forward and Inverse Modeling of Depth-of-Field Effects in Background-Oriented Schlieren

Joseph P. Molnar, Elijah J. LaLonde, Christopher S. Combs, Olivier Leon, David Donjat, Samuel J. Grauer

AIAA Journal · 2024 · 10.2514/1.J064095

Why: 真实 BOS/BOST forward model 不只是 thin-ray/pinhole；有限光圈和景深会造成 blur 与系统误差。这个 cone-ray 模型非常适合 M3C 真实数据质量控制和 geometry sanity check。

Extract: cone-ray vs thin-ray、finite aperture / f-number、depth-of-field blur、neural inverse model、total variation / Euler-equation regularization、真实实验 forward-model mismatch。

Sayaka Ichihara, Masato Yamagishi, Yuta Kurashina, Masanori Ota, Yoshiyuki Tagawa

Ultrasonics / arXiv · 2025 · 10.1016/j.ultras.2025.107614

Adaptively balanced Poisson-constrained physics-informed neural networks for robust displacement integration in background-oriented Schlieren

Hao Liu, Hongzhe Wang, Yang Song, Yunjing Ji, Jiancheng Lai, Zhenhua Li

Journal of the Optical Society of America A · 2026 · 10.1364/JOSAA.581983

Why: 把 BOS 位移场积分成物理场时容易受噪声、边界和 Poisson 约束权重影响；这篇给出 adaptive loss balancing 的 PINN 子模块，适合做本科可复现小题。

Extract: Poisson-constrained PINN、adaptive loss weights、displacement integration、noise robustness、BOS preprocessing 与 NeRIF/BOST forward-model 前处理边界。

Optics Express · 2024 · 10.1364/OE.536550

Why: 物理增强神经网络 + forward imaging model 的体火焰 tomography 代表作；和 NeRIF 同样强调物理投影一致性而非纯监督学习。

AIAA Journal / OSF preprint · 2021 · 10.2514/1.J060218

Why: NeRIF 参考文献直接引用的 BOS 图像处理方法；适合补 optical-flow family 的高级位移估计背景。

Extract: wavelet optical flow、multi-scale displacement、BOS image processing、和传统 PIV-style correlation 的差异。

Assimilating mean velocity fields of a shockwave-boundary layer interaction from background-oriented schlieren measurements using physics-informed neural networks

L. Rohlfs, J. Weiss

Physics of Fluids · 2024 · 10.1063/5.0208040

Why: 用 PINNs 从 BOS 测量同化 shockwave-boundary-layer interaction 平均速度场，是 PIV-BOST/physics-informed BOS 的重要速度场外延。

Extract: BOS-to-velocity assimilation, PDE residuals, SBLI benchmark, measurement constraints, and how velocity-field objectives differ from NeRIF refractive-index reconstruction.

Indoor Airflow Imaging Using Physics-Informed Background-Oriented Schlieren Tomography

Arjun Teh, Wael H. Ali, Joshua Rapp, Hassan Mansour

ICASSP 2025 / arXiv / MERL Technical Report · 2025 · 10.1109/ICASSP49660.2025.10889412

Extract: single-view BOS tomography、projector-camera setup、improved ray tracing、physics-based rendering loss、PINN regularization、buoyancy-driven indoor airflow。

support BOS uncertainty cached PDF

Uncertainty amplification due to density/refractive index gradients in background-oriented schlieren experiments

Lalit K. Rajendran, Sally M. Bane, Pavlos P. Vlachos

Experiments in Fluids / arXiv / OSTI · 2020 · 10.1007/s00348-020-02978-8

Why: BOST/NeRIF 不是只比 RMSE；强折射率梯度会放大位移不确定度，这正是火焰/高速流真实数据里的风险。

Extract: Cramer-Rao lower bound、dot blur、amplification ratio、uncertainty map、experiment design。

support BOS uncertainty online link

Quantifying numerical uncertainty in background-oriented schlieren

Pranjal Anand, Jiacheng Zhang, Lalit K. Rajendran, Sally P. M. Bane, Pavlos P. Vlachos

Experiments in Fluids · 2024 · 10.1007/s00348-023-03734-4

Why: 补足 BOS 不确定度的数值积分部分；M3C 质量控制不能只看模型 RMSE，也要区分随机误差、数值误差和梯度尖锐度导致的 bias。

Extract: Richardson extrapolation、density-gradient integration、random plus numerical uncertainty、bias error、grid-level residuals、experimental BOS uncertainty。

Subpixel-accurate real-time BOS measurement via GPU-optimized optical flow algorithms

Borui Zheng, Shixian Yang, Jiayin Chen, Hanyang Shen, Wengang Zhang, Liangyue Ji

Experiments in Fluids / Research Square preprint · 2026 · 10.1007/s00348-026-04277-0

Why: 实时 BOS 工程化方向；适合发展成 OpenCV/PyTorch/CUDA 小工具，而不是硬啃完整 4D BOST。

Extract: DIS/Farneback, optimized optical-flow pipeline, subpixel interpolation, real-time display, 30 FPS engineering constraints, how image-layer displacement baselines support PIV-BOST.

Bertrand Mercier, Tom Lacassagne

Flow Measurement and Instrumentation · 2023 · 10.1016/j.flowmeasinst.2023.102395

Why: 本科若要讨论实验误差和系统设计，必须知道背景距离、光路、位移估计和 least-squares 参数怎样影响 BOS 灵敏度。

Extract: experiment design rules、least-squares displacement、sensitivity/resolution trade-off、参数选择。

Simple yet powerful

Weiwei Cai, Zongyin Yang, Zhipei Sun, Tawfique Hasan

Nature Photonics · 2024 · 10.1038/s41566-024-01470-7

Why: 补 OERF 微型/计算光谱仪旁支。OERF 官网源码把该条写成 “Miniaturization of optical spectrometers: simple yet powerful” 且 DOI 似为占位；Crossref 核到正式题名为 “Simple yet powerful”。

Extract: computational spectrometer context, hardware miniaturization narrative, spectro-polarimetric sensing, device-algorithm co-design, why this is a side branch rather than the He/BOST mainline.

Combustion and Flame · 2025 · 10.1016/j.combustflame.2024.113864

Broadband miniaturized spectrometers with a van der Waals tunnel diode

Md Gius Uddin, Susobhan Das, Abde Mayeen Shafi, Lei Wang, Xiaoqi Cui, Fedor Nigmatulin, Faisal Ahmed, Andreas C. Liapis, Weiwei Cai, Zongyin Yang, Harri Lipsanen, Tawfique Hasan, Hoon Hahn Yoon, Zhipei Sun

Nature Communications · 2024 · 10.1038/s41467-024-44702-8

Why: OERF 计算光谱仪方向的 2024 高水平补充；它把 van der Waals 器件响应、宽带谱重构和计算算法结合起来，适合作为“硬件编码 + 反演重构”旁支。

Extract: van der Waals tunnel diode、MoS2/black phosphorus、500-1600 nm broadband reconstruction、peak wavelength accuracy、computational spectrometer。

Berthold K. P. Horn, Brian G. Schunck

Artificial Intelligence · 1981 · 10.1016/0004-3702(81)90024-2

Qianyi Wu, Jie Hu, Hongki Lee, Junxiang Zhao, Ming Lei, Zhaowei Liu

Optica · 2026 · 10.1364/OPTICA.577676

Why: 与 NeRIF 同属折射率场重构大方向，但路线偏 snapshot parallel imaging。它能帮你理解“硬件并行采样 + 折射率层析”的另一种解法，适合写 related work，不替代 He/BOST 主线。

Extract: snapshot parallel imaging, refractive-index tomography, hardware multiplexing, volumetric reconstruction, ambiguity and calibration constraints, contrast with neural refractive-index fields.

Crossref and Optica DOI verified; Optica metadata indicates VOR open-access license, but automated PDF retrieval is blocked by Optica validation, so the public site keeps the DOI/official entry and does not cache a PDF.

论文详情页Publisher / source
PDF / DOI
support BOS wavefront / aberration measurement online link

Wavefront aberration measurements based on the Background Oriented Schlieren method

Dominik Werner Wolf, Boris Thielbeer, Markus Ulrich, Alexander Braun

Measurement: Sensors · 2025 · 10.1016/j.measen.2024.101509

Why: 把 BOS 从“流场密度/折射率”扩展到 wavefront aberration 测量。对 M3C 系统误差、光路畸变和 PIV-BOST 折射补偿很有帮助。

Extract: wavefront aberration, BOS displacement to optical path distortion, calibration target, uncertainty, relation between refractive-index gradients and imaging-system correction.

Crossref and OpenAlex indicate Elsevier Gold OA / CC BY, but no stable redistribution-safe PDF URL was obtained in command-line checks; public site keeps the ScienceDirect entry only.

support BOS robustness / shock detection online link

Impact of atmospheric turbulence on shock detection using background-oriented Schlieren

Shiwei Liu, Yichong Ren, Haiping Mei, Zhiwei Tao, Xiaoxuan Ma, Ruizhong Rao

Optics Express · 2025 · 10.1364/OE.567012

Why: BOS/BOST 在真实高速流里会受背景扰动、湍流和成像噪声影响。这篇适合补“shock detection 和环境扰动如何影响位移场”的鲁棒性讨论。

Extract: atmospheric turbulence, shock detection, BOS image degradation, false positives/negatives, robustness metrics, implications for high-speed-flow BOST data quality.

Crossref indicates Optics Express VOR-OA; automated DOI/PDF access redirects to Optica/Radware validation in this environment, so no local PDF is cached.

Applied Optics / arXiv · 2026 · 10.1364/AO.597572

Why: 2026 Applied Optics / arXiv optical-system support paper: it replaces the external knife-edge in classical schlieren with the camera lens internal aperture, keeping schlieren sensitivity while simplifying alignment and reducing components. Useful for M3C system-health reporting, low-cost optics intuition, and explaining how schlieren differs from BOS/BOST hardware.

Extract: Extract the cutoff-element role of a knife-edge, lens-aperture-as-cutoff geometry, sensitivity/contrast tradeoff, alignment constraints, cost/complexity reduction, and what this can and cannot transfer to BOS/BOST background-pattern setups.

cached open arXiv PDF; arXiv page links formal Applied Optics reference and DOI 10.1364/AO.597572. Official Optica page is used as DOI/publisher confirmation; public cache uses arXiv version.

support BOS displacement / Fourier demodulation online link

Background oriented schlieren technique with fast Fourier demodulation for measuring large density-gradient fields of fluids

Takaaki Shimazaki, Sayaka Ichihara, Yoshiyuki Tagawa

Experimental Thermal and Fluid Science · 2022 · 10.1016/j.expthermflusci.2022.110598

Why: Open-access BOS displacement-processing support paper: introduces Fast Checkerboard Demodulation (FCD) for BOS, replacing random-dot cross-correlation/PIV-style displacement extraction with Fourier-space phase demodulation on periodic patterns. Valuable for understanding why PIV/BOS displacement can fail in strong density-gradient fields and for designing M3A/M3C displacement-quality checks.

Extract: Extract FCD-BOS principle, periodic/checkerboard background requirements, Fourier-domain phase-change displacement estimate, underwater-shock validation, reported measurement range versus conventional PIV-BOS, and limits imposed by grid size and Fourier-space separation.

ScienceDirect article page verified as Open access under a Creative Commons license, but direct PDF routes returned 403/handshake/406 in command-line checks. Kept as official open article link-only; no local PDF cached.

Why: Root paper for natural-background BOS: it explains how outdoor or scene texture can replace a printed dot background, which is useful for low-cost demonstrations, field deployment, background-quality checks and reference-free BOS discussions.

Extract: Natural background requirements, outdoor schlieren imaging geometry, background texture/contrast constraints, leak/blast/thermal-flow visualization use cases, and why natural-background BOS is a system-design neighbor rather than a BOST reconstruction baseline.

Why: Reference-free natural-background BOS is a practical field-deployment example: it uses scene texture and optical-flow style processing to visualize building-wall air leakage without a lab dot background. IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging · 2018 · 10.1109/TMI.2018.2799231

Why: Learned primal-dual reconstruction embeds a possibly nonlinear forward operator into an unrolled reconstruction network. It is the canonical warning against treating tomography as generic voxel post-processing.

Extract: Extract primal/dual state updates, forward/adjoint calls, raw-measurement interface, unroll depth, training cost and comparison with filtered backprojection.

On Learned Operator Correction in Inverse Problems

Sebastian Lunz, Andreas Hauptmann, Tanja Tarvainen, Carola-Bibiane Schonlieb, Simon Arridge

SIAM Journal on Imaging Sciences · 2021 · 10.1137/20M1338460

Why: This work treats mismatched forward physics with paired forward- and adjoint-space corrections, making it a precise neighbor for future BOST calibration or curved-ray model mismatch rather than a generic architecture citation.

Extract: Extract why correcting only the forward map is insufficient, the paired correction contract, convergence assumptions and the limited-view photoacoustic experiment. Compare those assumptions with a real BOST Jacobian/VJP.

Official SIAM article and PMC records identify the paper as Creative Commons 4.0. A 36-page, 2.84 MB PDF from OuluREPO was cached and verified on 2026-07-16; SHA-256 d4d05b06261af1451b5c2c800630d46bcbff2e5fcd7e5847ca266c908c44c266.

Computed Tomography: Algorithms, Insight, and Just Enough Theory

Per Christian Hansen, Jakob Jorgensen, William R. B. Lionheart

SIAM Fundamentals of Algorithms · 2021 · 10.1137/1.9781611976670

Why: This is the most useful pedagogical bridge from undergraduate linear algebra to FBP, algebraic iterative reconstruction, regularization, optimization, SVD and incomplete-data effects needed before designing a learned BOST solver.

Extract: Prioritize Chapters 9-13: system matrix, algebraic iterations, regularization and first-order optimization. Reproduce a small AIR Tools-style experiment rather than treating CT physics as identical to BOST.

The Troublesome Kernel: On Hallucinations, No Free Lunches, and the Accuracy-Stability Tradeoff in Inverse Problems

Nina M. Gottschling, Vegard Antun, Anders C. Hansen, Ben Adcock

SIAM Review · 2025 · 10.1137/23M1568739

Why: The paper links hallucination, instability and unpredictable generalization to nontrivial or ill-conditioned forward kernels. It supplies the theoretical motivation for v3k-C worst-layout and tail-harm reporting.

Extract: Extract the accuracy-stability tradeoff, kernel assumptions, failure constructions and limits of empirical evidence. Connect them to sparse-view BOST without claiming the synthetic tail audit proves the theorems experimentally.

Official SIAM Review volume, pages, authors and DOI verified on 2026-07-15. Publisher/metadata link only; no restricted full text is cached.

Variable Projection for Nonlinear Least Squares Problems

Dianne P. O'Leary, Bert W. Rust

Computational Optimization and Applications · 2012 · 10.1007/s10589-012-9492-9

Why: This is the practical bridge from Golub-Pereyra variable projection to the v5b profile objective: eliminate many linear field variables while optimizing a few nonlinear optical parameters.

Extract: Reproduce one separable least-squares example, derive the reduced Jacobian, record constraint handling, and separate optimization efficiency from parameter identifiability.

NIST publication metadata and the authors' University of Maryland manuscript/software page were verified on 2026-07-16. The 31-page author manuscript is cached from the University of Maryland source; no publisher-only PDF is cached.

Blind Calibration for Compressed Sensing by Convex Optimization

Remi Gribonval, Gilles Chardon, Laurent Daudet

arXiv preprint arXiv:1111.7248 · 2011 · no DOI

Why: It is a direct warning against treating shared gain calibration as novel or automatically identifiable: multiple unknown signals, structural priors, and an explicit normalization gauge are all required.

Extract: Extract the bilinear scale ambiguity, trace normalization, number-of-signals phase transition, and why sparse compressed sensing assumptions do not transfer automatically to dense reaction fields.

The official arXiv record and full text were verified on 2026-07-16. It is linked as prior art; no local redistribution claim is made.

Structural and Practical Identifiability Analysis of Partially Observed Dynamical Models by Exploiting the Profile Likelihood

A. Raue, C. Kreutz, T. Maiwald, J. Bachmann, M. Schilling, U. Klingmuller, J. Timmer

Bioinformatics · 2009 · 10.1093/bioinformatics/btp358

Why: It supplies the clean distinction between structural and practical non-identifiability that v5b needs when local Schur curvature and a complete profile curve disagree.

Extract: Extract flat versus bounded profiles, likelihood confidence thresholds, nuisance re-optimization, and the experimental-design implication that structural non-identifiability needs qualitatively new measurements.

Learning about Physical Parameters: The Importance of Model Discrepancy

Jenny Brynjarsdottir, Anthony O'Hagan

Inverse Problems · 2014 · 10.1088/0266-5611/30/11/114007

Why: This is the key calibration warning for finite-aperture BOST: an imperfect renderer can force a physical parameter to absorb model discrepancy while still improving data fit.

Extract: Extract the parameter-discrepancy confounding examples and translate them into native-scale, shared-scale and effective-aperture audits without assuming a Gaussian-process discrepancy is the right BOST solution.

Crossref/IOP DOI metadata and bibliographic records were verified on 2026-07-16. Publisher link only; no restricted PDF is cached.

Learned ReSeSOp for Solving Inverse Problems with Inexact Forward Operator

Mathias S. Feinler, Bernadette N. Hahn

Inverse Problems · 2025 · 10.1088/1361-6420/adef73

Why: This recent method targets iterative reconstruction with inexact forward operators and learned inexactness levels, making it a stronger comparator than a generic FNO for any proposed BOST discrepancy-aware solver.

Extract: Extract the ReSeSOp stripe construction, learned inexactness estimator, dependency assumptions, dynamic imaging experiments, and which parts require known subproblem structure absent in current BOST data.

Crossref verifies the 2025 Inverse Problems article and CC BY 4.0 license; the official IOP PDF endpoint returned a bot-challenge HTML to command-line download. The verified arXiv manuscript is linked online and no invalid file is cached.

Generalized Cross-Validation as a Method for Choosing a Good Ridge Parameter

Gene H. Golub, Michael Heath, Grace Wahba

Technometrics · 1979 · 10.1080/00401706.1979.10489751

Why: v5c selected the largest kappa in all six blocks. GCV is the classical starting point for correcting residual scores by the effective degrees of freedom rather than rewarding shrinkage without a complexity account.

Extract: Derive the ridge hat matrix, trace-based effective degrees of freedom, GCV denominator and invariance assumptions. Implement it per candidate radius before opening any new synthetic lock.

Official journal DOI landing page verified on 2026-07-16. Link-only entry; no subscription PDF is cached or published.

On the Solution of Functional Equations by the Method of Regularization

V. A. Morozov

USSR Computational Mathematics and Mathematical Physics · 1968 · no DOI

Why: The discrepancy principle is the direct alternative when noise is externally calibrated: stop decreasing the residual once it is commensurate with the noise, instead of choosing the strongest shrinkage that wins cross-view prediction.

Extract: Extract the noise-bound assumption, discrepancy equation, existence/selection logic and the difference between nominal simulation sigma and flow-off experimental covariance.

MathNet bibliographic/original-source page verified on 2026-07-16. Link-only entry; no uncertain-license local copy is published.

Blur Identification by the Method of Generalized Cross-Validation

Stanley J. Reeves, Russell M. Mersereau

IEEE Transactions on Image Processing · 1992 · 10.1109/83.148604

Why: This is the closest classical collision for jointly identifying an imaging blur/model and regularization. It prevents claiming that joint optical-parameter and ridge selection is itself novel.

Extract: Extract how GCV couples blur, image model and regularization, what assumptions make the identification possible, and which BOST-specific camera/shared-field constraints remain outside that result.

PubMed/IEEE bibliographic metadata and DOI verified on 2026-07-16. Link-only entry; no restricted IEEE PDF is cached.

Joint Calibration and Tomography Based on Separable Least Squares Approach with Constraints on Linear and Non-Linear Parameters

Venkata Pathuri-Bhuvana, Stefan Schuster, Andreas Och

28th European Signal Processing Conference (EUSIPCO) · 2021 · 10.23919/Eusipco47968.2020.9287717

Why: It directly combines constrained variable projection, tomographic reconstruction and sensor-position calibration. This is the closest prior-art warning against presenting low-dimensional calibration plus tomography as a mathematical first.

Extract: Extract the separable objective, constraints, calibration variables, simulated acoustic setup and the boundary between sensor-position calibration and BOST effective-aperture calibration.
